mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains below its recent peak, the discount rate on equity cash flows eases and S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. This signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with positive forward returns or if equities fall despite a clear easing path.
failure_condition: If triggered on monthly FEDFUNDS declines, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of cases or the average forward return is not above the unconditional mean, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration implies tighter real policy expectations and lower equity multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next month after inflation re-accelerates. The claim fails if rising CPI does not weaken equities after the lagged rate-response window.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if, after CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above its prior 12-month average, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in at least 60% of cases or the mean return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a soft-landing threshold signals weakening labor income and lower cyclical earnings, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is invalid if unemployment increases without a later equity drawdown.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months but 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than half of cases, or if Rank IC is near zero, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal demand and supports revenue growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal fails when sales strength does not translate into positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth months are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative months, or if the forward-return spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO together with a rebound in HOUST signals improving supply-side capacity and cyclically stronger growth, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim is falsified if industrial and housing recovery do not co-move with later equity strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous INDPRO and HOUST strength fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal loses directional accuracy over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently strong PAYEMS growth indicates expanding payroll income and consumption capacity, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next week. The mechanism fails if payroll gains do not translate into near-term equity follow-through.
failure_condition: If months with positive PAYEMS growth do not produce higher 5-day forward S&P 500 returns than months with flat or negative payroll growth, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and lower earnings revisions, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next quarter. This claim is refuted if GDP deceleration is not followed by equity weakness.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth below 1% does not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if average forward return remains positive after such slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL stays elevated while FEDFUNDS is also above 4.5%, markets should expect restrictive real policy and lower valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The hypothesis fails if high inflation combined with tight policy does not weaken equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 6-month median
failure_condition: If the joint high-FEDFUNDS/high-CPI regime does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations, or the mean forward return is not below zero, reject the hypothesis.
